pi_items.source: FEMA OpenFEMA API query confirmed California has fire disaster declarations
API response shows incidentType='Fire' for disasters DR-4856, DR-4619, DR-4610
FEMA API confirmed all three recent California fire disasters are declarationType='DR' (Disaster Relief/Major Disaster)

FEMA OpenFEMA API confirmed at least 3 Major Disaster declarations exist for Fire incidents in California. As of 2026-03-06, the 3 most recent are: DR-4856 (2025-01-08), DR-4619 (2021-09-12), DR-4610 (2021-08-24)
Verified that FEMA disaster pages are accessible at https://www.fema.gov/disaster/{disasterNumber}
This is a file path specification; cannot verify without task execution
This is a naming specification for Firefox bookmarks; cannot verify correct name without authoritative guidance

privileged_info_summary: The FEMA disaster database (accessed via fema.gov/disaster and OpenFEMA API at api.fema.gov) contains real government disaster declaration data. California has multiple Major Disaster declarations for Fire/Wildfire incidents. As of March 2026, the 3 most recent are: (1) DR-4856 declared 2025-01-08 for 'WILDFIRES AND STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS' with incident period 2025-01-07 to 2025-01-31; (2) DR-4619 declared 2021-09-12 for 'WILDFIRES' with incident period 2021-08-14 to 2021-10-21; (3) DR-4610 declared 2021-08-24 for 'WILDFIRES' with incident period 2021-08-14 to 2021-10-21. The FEMA API provides standardized fields including disasterNumber (numeric), femaDeclarationString (formatted as 'DR-####-CA'), declarationTitle, declarationDate, incidentBeginDate, and incidentEndDate. These declarations are accessible through fema.gov/disaster/{number}.
